design_philosophy.core_loop: Helicopter bombing runs against island factories while managing escalating enemy counterattacks; balance speed (prevent factory development) against survival (defend against incoming threats)
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Time pressure vs. defensive survival—factories develop new weaponry if left unmolested too long, creating a race against technological escalation
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Piloting a combat helicopter on a precision strike mission against an enemy empire's industrial infrastructure from a mobile aircraft carrier base
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Systems-driven gameplay with visible infrastructure interdependence (supply boats between islands); factories actively grow and advance rather than remain static targets. Player must act as both aggressor and defender. Emphasizes reactive strategy over reflexive shooting.

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Resource management and targeting priority determine outcome; player must decide bombing sequence to prevent escalation while managing fuel and ammunition constraints

progression.unlock_mechanism: Hidden island available for reload/resupply once discovered by player exploration
replayability_hooks: Variable factory advancement states depending on bombing speed

world_building_techniques: Enemy faction establishment (Bungeling Empire as recurring Broderbund villains)
Supply chain visualization via moving boats between islands
Progressive threat escalation reflecting industrial development
Failure state narrative: empire develops war machine to invade Earth

faction_indicators: Bungeling Empire as named antagonist faction
Aircraft carrier as player home base/faction anchor
Supply logistics between islands indicate enemy coordination
environmental_storytelling: Visible supply boats traveling between islands show enemy infrastructure interconnection
Factory growth and development visible as gameplay escalation
Hidden island serves as secret resupply point suggesting player agency and world discovery
Aircraft carrier as mobile operations base conveys tactical naval warfare setting

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Raid on Bungeling Bay NES)
